Context Source: Master of Business Administration (MBA) - Boston International College (https://bostoncollege.edu.np/master-of-business-administration-mba/) | Information: Master of Business Administration (MBA) is a general management degree taught from a global perspective that prepares students for a range of management careers. MBA offers a comprehensive understanding of business and management functions. This course helps students to learn leadership and entrepreneurship qualities, and develop their problem-solving and analytical skills, which are much required in today’s competitive and complex business world to be a successful management leader and entrepreneur. The MBA in BIC is affiliated to Pokhara University. It is a two-year program with six trimesters. For successful completion of the degree, a student is required to earn 66 credit hours by successfully completing 27 courses and 4 practicums and graduate seminars. Of the total 27 courses, students are required to take 7 foundation and analytical tool courses (18 credits), 10 cores and functional courses (20 credits), 2 capstone courses (6 credits), 4 concentration/ specialization courses (8 credits), 2 electives (4 credits) and graduate research project, internship, seminars, and other projects (10 credits).

Context Source: Master of Business Administration (MBA) - Boston International College (https://bostoncollege.edu.np/master-of-business-administration-mba/) | Information: In addition to the foundation and core courses, students are also required to choose a specialization stream and select 4 courses in that stream totaling to 8 credits. The courses in the specialization area intend to develop a high degree of competence in the chosen area and are offered in the 4th and 5th trimesters/terms. At Boston International College (BIC), we strive to be the premier institution for MBA education in Nepal. Our distinguished faculty, hailing from diverse industrial backgrounds both within Nepal and internationally, brings invaluable real-world experience into the classroom. We take pride in our 92% MBA graduation rate, reflecting our commitment to academic excellence. Beyond academics, our Personality Development Classes equip students with vital hard and soft skills, fostering career advancement. Additionally, our International Student Exchange program and internal internships provide global exposure, while our dedicated placement support ensures 100% job placements for our graduates.

Context Source: QAA - Boston International College (https://bostoncollege.edu.np/qaa/) | Information: Boston International College (BIC), a UGC-accredited academic institution affiliated to Pokhara University, Nepal was established in 2009 with the aim of providing world-class professional management and IT-based academic courses at the graduate and undergraduate levels in Nepal. Since its inception, the college has been offering undergraduate programs, including Bachelor of Business Administration (BBA), Bachelor of Business Administration in Banking and Insurance (BBA-BI), BBA-Finance, and Bachelor of Computer Information System (BCIS/BCSIT). Additionally, it has been running a Master of Business Administration program since 2017. In its commitment to expanding its educational offerings, the college has introduced a Bachelor in Hotel Management (BHM) program from August 2024.Over our 17-year academic journey, we have proudly celebrated four gold medallists and 162 dean’s-list students. Our faculty comprises over 40 national educators and 22+ international scholars, supported by numerous academic and industry collaborations. Beyond academics, we have nurtured 27 student-led startup ideas, seed-funded 5 projects with seed money from college and UGC’s Entrepreneurship Support Program (ESP). Our students learn pragmatic management skills through seven annual mega-events, 10 clubs, and over 6 outreach and community engagement programs under student leadership, creating diverse platforms to cultivate future management leaders. Similarly, the Project Exhibition program features more than 10 projects every year from BCSIT Students in the 6th and 8th Semesters. Recently, the college has received Quality Assurance and Accreditation (QAA) certification by meeting all the benchmarks set by the University Grants Commission, Nepal.
